findings.title: Tier 3 can route to an arbitrary target_doc path
findings.impact: A malformed or prompt-injected Tier 3 classifier response can place ../../package.json, another existing file, or another non-canonical path into the routing decision. The save handler later resolves that string with path.join and reads/writes the resulting target if it exists.
findings.recommendedFix: Validate Tier 3 target_doc against buildTarget(category, packetLevel, likelyPhaseAnchor), or an explicit allow-list of canonical target docs, and enforce resolved-path containment before any save handler uses the route.

findings.title: Handover notes that include repository state can still be refused
findings.impact: The packet fixed git diff/list memories/force re-index confusion, but a realistic handover line such as 'Current state ... Repository state is dirty ... Next session ...' still gives handover_state 0.84 and drop 0.92, triggering escalation and refusal instead of preserving the stop-state note.
findings.recommendedFix: Treat repository-state/tool-telemetry language as soft when strong handover language is present, or remove it from hard drop scoring unless transcript/wrapper cues also appear.

findings.title: Regression tests miss the hard-wrapper-plus-handover case and only assert category for refreshed prototypes
findings.impact: The focused test covers one exact soft-command string and asserts refusal=false there, but it does not cover repository-state/tool-telemetry handover text. The prototype boundary test only checks category, so a handover_state decision that is refused to manual review would still satisfy that assertion.
findings.recommendedFix: Add tests for repository-state and tool-telemetry mixed with current-state/next-session language, and assert target doc plus refusal=false for refreshed handover prototypes.

findings.title: Explicit routeAs='drop' produces an internally inconsistent non-refusal decision
findings.impact: The override path forces refusal=false for every routeAs value, including drop, while buildTarget(drop) returns a refuse-to-route target. Downstream code currently catches category === drop, but audit entries and direct router consumers see contradictory state.
findings.recommendedFix: Special-case routeAs drop so refusal=true, overrideable=false, and confidenceBand=refuse, or reject drop as an explicit override at schema/runtime boundaries.

findings.title: Tier 3 prompt sends unbounded raw chunk text even though normalized routing text is clipped
findings.impact: Tier 1/Tier 2 operate on normalized text clipped to 2048 characters, but Tier 3 prompt construction includes input.chunk_text without the same bound. Large save chunks can produce oversized Tier 3 requests or increase prompt-injection surface.
findings.recommendedFix: Pass the clipped normalized text, or an explicit bounded raw preview, into the Tier 3 prompt and record the original char count separately.
